items.note: Pictograph of water flowing between banks. As a left-side radical it becomes 氵(three drops) — seen in 海 (sea), 泳ぐ (to swim).
Pictograph of a flame. As a bottom radical it flattens into 灬 (four dots) — seen in 魚 (fish) and 黒い (black).
Pictograph of a mountain with three peaks. Used as a radical in many nature and geography kanji.
Pictograph of a river — three vertical lines represent streams of flowing water between banks.
Pictograph of a person walking. As a left-side radical it becomes 亻— seen in 休 (rest: person 亻 leaning on a tree 木), 体 (body).
Pictograph of the sun with a center mark. One of the most frequent radicals — seen in 明 (bright: 日+月), 時 (time), 本 (Japan = 日本, 'origin of the sun').
Pictograph of a crescent moon. Also doubles as the 'flesh/meat' radical (from 肉) in body-related kanji like 胃 (stomach) and 脳 (brain).
Pictograph of a tree: top strokes are branches, bottom strokes are roots. 木+木 = 林 (grove), 木+木+木 = 森 (forest). As left-side radical: 木.
木 (tree) + a stroke at the base marking the root → 'origin, basis'. Extended to mean 'book'. Also used in 日本 (Japan = origin of the sun).
先 (ahead/before) + 生 (life/birth) → one who was born before you → a teacher or guide.
友 contains two 又 (right hand) strokes — two hands reaching toward each other. 達 adds the nuance of companions who travel together.
目 (eye) + 儿 (legs/person below) → an eye with legs: to go and look. The eye radical 目 is the key semantic element.
